posture: Watch (ran clean) — no fresh acute trigger surfaced across the free sources in the last 24 hours, extending the quiet weekend cycle. No new political, delivery, supply-chain, market, safety or cyber signal cleared the NSW-REZ relevance bar today, so no items are logged. Standing exposures are unchanged and carried forward: the New England REZ Access Scheme draft declaration remains unconfirmed as released (EnergyCo's access-scheme page is still marked 'Updated 27 February 2026' and shows only the March-2026 anticipated timeline, now several days past its end-June target); EnergyCo's New England corridor field-investigation campaign runs July–September across contested landholdings while the Walcha/High Country opposition and the Coalition's pledge to cap the zone and scrap the Bayswater 500kV link stay live; and the DPHI investigation into ACEREZ's destruction of an Aboriginal rock shelter on the Central-West Orana build is still open.
summary: A full scan of the free source register returned no new NSW-REZ-relevant development in the last 24 hours. Every thread that surfaced today (the New England access-scheme timing, the July–September field-investigation program, the Walcha 22,000-signature petition and Coalition pledge, the ACEREZ heritage investigation, the developer-consortium inland-region push, and the commencement of AER-determined quarterly Transgrid enabling-project payments from 1 July) is either a prior-digest entry with no fresh movement or dated/procedural material outside the 24-hour news window, and is deduplicated out per the writing rules. Today is therefore published with empty sections as a continuity / 'ran clean' marker. The most material standing signal remains procedural: EnergyCo's target of releasing the New England REZ Access Scheme draft declaration by end June has still not been confirmed as exhibited on the agency's access-scheme page, keeping a low-grade schedule-slippage watch live ahead of the end-September ministerial declaration and the March 2027 state-election backdrop.
